raw_consumer_statement: I contacted Barclay 's Priceline XXXX  Rewards Line In XX/XX/XXXX on a Friday to report a {$1000.00} Cash Advance with charges and a {$100.00} cash advance with charges that was put on my XXXX  card. I have never done a cash advance and was upset that they did not contact me. The card was cancelled and another card was issued. I did not receive the card and decided to go into my account to look at the statement. There were over 20 charges in small increments coming from stores in XXXX. I had not been out of the country since XXXX, and these charges were for little stores and a motel that had to be purchased in person. I called XXXX XXXX department and reported this immediately. They cancelled my card on a Saturday and issued another card. The next day on Sunday XXXX called me to ask me about suspicious charges on the card that they just issued on Saturday. I told them no, so they cancelled that card and issued another card. They called again on Sunday to report suspicious charges on the newest card and I told them to completely close my XXXX account. Since that time, I have received multiple letters telling me I am responsible for the charges in XXXX, and further stated they would report me to XXXX  if I did not pay them with my next credit card statement. They did state that I was not responsible for the cash advances. This has been going on for several months. I still have the ( 2 ) credit cards attached to the original mailing papers that arrived together that were never activated. XXXX XXXX unit stated that the individual charging was using the XXXXXXXX XXXX was able to change my passwords and access my account. These cards have never left my house. I feel XXXX has caused me undo stress and will not take responsibility for the lack of security on their accounts. I will never use XXXX XXXX XXXX again.
